● Sudden Ionospheric Disturbance (SID) space weather monitors can pick up X-class, M-class and a few strong C-class flares.
○ SID monitors measure the effects of solar flares on Earth's ionosphere. They do this by tracking changes in very low frequency (VLF) transmissions as they bounce off the ionosphere.
Classification and Characteristics of Different Classes of Solar Flares:

Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Magnets are used in which of the following devices?
1. Credit cards
2. Computer hard drives
3. Induction geysers
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● A magnet is a material or object that produces a magnetic field.
● A credit card contains a magnetic strip. Scientifically, when two magnets come in contact with each other, they can lose their magnetic power. The card machine reads the personal information stored in the magnetic strip when the card is swiped. So, point 1 is correct.
● Many computers use magnets to store data on hard drives. Magnets alter the direction of magnetic material on a hard disk in segments that then represent computer data. Later, computers read the direction of each segment of the magnetic material to “read” the data. So, point 2 is correct.
● Induction geysers are a new technology that uses the magnetic induction process to heat water. So, point 3 is correct.
○ Induction heating is the process of heating a metal (or any material that conducts electricity) by inducing an electric current through it.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to Antimatter:
1. They do not have any mass or electric charge.
2. They can be created both naturally and artificially.
3. They always act against the laws of gravitational forces.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Antimatter is a substance composed of subatomic particles that have the mass, electric charge and magnetic moment of the electrons, protons and neutrons of ordinary matter but for which the electric charge and magnetic moment are opposite in sign. So, statement 1 is not correct.
○ The antimatter particles corresponding to electrons, protons and neutrons are called positrons (e+), antiprotons (p) and antineutrons (n); collectively they are referred to as antiparticles.
○ The electrical properties of antimatter being opposite to those of ordinary matter, the positron has a positive charge and the antiproton a negative charge; the antineutron, though electrically neutral, has a magnetic moment opposite in sign to that of the neutron.
● There are naturally produced antiparticles made sporadically throughout the universe. But when matter and antimatter meet, they annihilate each other and produce energy, meaning that in a matter dominated cosmos such as ours, antimatter doesn't stick around very long. Humans have also artificially created antimatter particles using ultra-high-speed collisions at huge particle accelerators such as the Large Hadron Collider, which is located outside Geneva and operated by CERN (the European Organization for Nuclear Research). So, statement 2 is correct.
● According to recent research, antihydrogen atoms, the antimatter counterpart of hydrogen, obey the same gravitational laws as conventional matter. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs of Bio-Imaging techniques and their applications:
Bio-Imaging Technique
 Application
Calcium Imaging
 Measurement of calcium levels in tissues
Diffusion-weighted Magnetic Resonance Imaging
 Mapping of the diffusion process of molecules, mainly water, in tissues
Gallium Imaging
 Visualisation of cells invisible to the naked eye
Bioluminescence Imaging
 Studying laboratory animals using luminescent protein
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?

so.t:
● Bio-imaging refers to a procedure in which there is no involvement of tools that can invade the skin or physically enter the body. It allows scientists to view biological functions in real-time. The purpose of bioimaging is to cause as minimal disruption to live processes as possible.
● Calcium Imaging is a microscopy technique to optically measure the calcium (Ca2+) status of an isolated cell, tissue or medium. Calcium imaging takes advantage of calcium indicators, fluorescent molecules that respond to the binding of Ca2+ ions by fluorescence properties. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● Diffusion-Weighted Magnetic Resonance Imaging (DW-MRI) is the use of specific MRI sequences as well as software that generates images from the resulting data that uses the diffusion of water molecules to generate contrast in MR images. It allows the mapping of the diffusion process of molecules, mainly water, in biological tissues, in vivo and non-invasively. So, pair 2 is correctly matched.
● Gallium Imaging is a type of nuclear scan that can find cancer, infection or inflammation in the body. A radiologist injects a small amount of a radioactive substance into the bloodstream. Gallium settles in areas of the body where there is inflammation or infection. A special camera locates the gallium and takes pictures. So, pair 3 is not correctly matched.
● Bioluminescence Imaging (BLI) is an experimental technique that uses light-emitting proteins (luciferases) found naturally in insects, bacteria and plants as optical reporters ideal for cell labelling in vivo. It is a technique for studying laboratory animals using luminescent protein. So, pair 4 is correctly matched. So, only three of the above pairs are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which one of the following monuments of India has this inscription engraved?
‘Hevam loksa hitmukhe ti, atha iyam natisu hevam’. Meaning, the welfare and happiness of humanity should always be ensured.

so.t:
● The Delhi Topra Ashokan Pillar is erected above the palace building at Feroz Shah Kotla in Delhi. It is 13 metres (43 feet) high (with one metre below the platform) and made of sandstone.
● The inscription is written in the Prakrit language, which is a fusion of Pali and Sanskrit.
● "Hevam lokasa hita-sukhe ti, ath iyam naatisu hevam” means "the welfare and happiness of humanity should always be ensured,"
● The quote is a part of the sixth edict, inscribed on the pillar. Ashokan Edicts were inscribed on pillars to spread the right doctrine and the code of life which speaks of kindness, forbearance, and concern for the welfare of people.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the Tattvabodhini Sabha, consider the following statements:
1. It was established by Surendranath Banerjee.
2. It aimed to spread the knowledge of the Shastras and the Vedantas among the people.
3. It worked for public welfare and the political awakening of the people against the British government.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Tattvabodhini Sabha was established in 1839 by Debendranath Tagore at Jorasanko, Calcutta (now Kolkata). For twenty years (1839-1859), the Sabha heavily influenced the region's social, political and cultural aspects. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● Debendranath Tagore created the Sabha to spread the knowledge and the truth of the Shastras and the Vedantas among the country's people. Its objectives were to prevent cultural intrusion from the West and protect the educated youths from being influenced by the Christian missionaries. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Tattvabodhini Sabha helped increase social consciousness in society by trying to eliminate religious dogmas. It continuously worked for public welfare and helped with people's political awakening against the British government. So, statement 3 is correct. So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Indian Oil Corporation's major refinery - Bhatinda

q.t: Which of the following combinations of industries and their locations in Punjab is INCORRECT?

so.t: Explanation: Option C is incorrect and therefore the right answer to this question. While Fortis Healthcare does have significant presence and hospitals in Mohali (particularly in the SAS Nagar area), its corporate headquarters is actually located in Gurugram (Gurgaon), Haryana, not in Punjab. Fortis does operate Fortis Hospital Mohali, which is a major healthcare facility, but this is a hospital location, not the corporate headquarters of the entire Fortis Healthcare chain. Option A is correct—Hero MotoCorp (formerly Hero Honda), the world's largest two-wheeler manufacturer, has its major manufacturing facility in Ludhiana. The company has deep roots in Punjab, having been founded by the Munjal family, and Ludhiana serves as one of its primary production hubs. Option B is also correct—Bathinda (also spelled Bhatinda) hosts a major refinery of Indian Oil Corporation (IOCL). The Guru Gobind Singh Refinery at Bathinda, commissioned in 2012, has a capacity of 11.3 million metric tonnes per annum and is a crucial petroleum facility in northern India. Option D is accurate as well—Punjab Tractors Limited (PTL), which manufactures Swaraj tractors, has its plant in Rajpuram, near Ludhiana. Ludhiana is indeed a major industrial hub of Punjab, known for bicycle manufacturing, auto parts, textiles, and engineering goods. Other important industrial centers in Punjab include Mohali (IT and pharmaceuticals), Jalandhar (sports goods), and Amritsar (textiles). Understanding industrial locations is important for Punjab's economic geography, especially given the state's challenge of industrial stagnation and the need for economic diversification beyond agriculture.

q.t:
With reference to the Rangpur Dhing Rebellion of 1783, consider the following statements:
1. It was organised against revenue farmers, who oppressed both the ryots and the zamindars.
2. The imposition of illegal taxes like batta was one of the factors for the rebellion.
3. J.D. Paterson headed the Rangpur Commission enquiring into the causes of the rebellion.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The Rangpur Dhing Rebellion of 1783 was a peasant uprising in the Rangpur district of Bengal. The rebellion began when peasants and zamindars took control of the Parganas of Kakina, Kazirhat and Tepa. The rebels attacked courts, looted grain and released prisoners
● Raja Devi Singh (a revenue farmer) was complicit in torturing the peasants both physically and psychologically. Devi Singh inflicted cruelty on both, the ryots and the zamindars, of the region. When his cruelty became unbearable, the ryots rose up in a rebellion. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The following were the causes of the revolt:
○ The imposition of an increase of Rs.162401 (appx) on the jama of 1780 on the people of Rangpur.
○ Instituting certain taxes for realising the revenue.
○ The imposition of illegal taxes: derinwallah, batta, hoonderan, rusum (‘rusoom’), mustajir (‘mustageer’) on the peasants. So, statement 2 is correct.
■ Batta was a payment given to British officers in India to cover expenses while on the march. It was originally introduced as a supplement to the military officers' salary.
● After the events of the dhing, the administration set up the Rangpur Commission under the leadership of J.D. Paterson. While he did his best, it appears the administration found some of his documents to be questionable. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Land revenue system started by Mughal emperor Akbar and continued by the British Government of India in the Bengal region
Fiscal system of the Government of British India that led to the establishment of liquor and opium shops
Social reform policy which favoured the use of the Urdu language in the administration
Commercial policy under which merchants were forced to sell their products to the British East India Company at a much lower price

q.t: The Abkari system in British India was a:

so.t:
● The Abkari system was a fiscal system of the Government of British India that led to the establishment of spirit distilleries, liquor and opium shops in large numbers of places, channelized in a way to reach the maximum number of people.
● S.G. Lavate of the Bombay provincial congress addressed the topic of the Abkari system in the 17th Bombay Provincial Congress held under the auspices of Sarvajanik Sabha of Poona, in Budhwar Peth, Poona. He argued that the British Government’s Abkari system inadvertently exposed the inherently coercive and corrupt character of the British administrative officers. He stated that India's reputation had faded in the earlier centuries due to the Abkari system that miserably failed to check the regulation of alcohol in society.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t: ‘Bhavani Mandir’, which articulates the idea of the nation as the mother-goddess, was published by:

so.t:
● Bhawani Mandir was a pamphlet written in 1905 by Aurobindo Ghose. It was written against the backdrop of the announcement of the partition of Bengal. It articulates the idea of the nation as the mother-goddess and the need for total devotion to her.
● He asserts that millions of Indians too must commit themselves to the rebirth and strengthening of the nation, in response to the appeal of the Nation-Goddess. In order to achieve this revival, Ghose proposed a revolutionary secret society “Bhawani Mandir”, to be located deep in the mountains. There, a band of patriotic ascetics would receive training and commit their lives selflessly to the service and liberation of the nation.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to slavery in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Charter Act, 1833 mandated the Governor-General to take measures to end slavery in India.
2. The Indian Slavery Act, 1843 banned the sale of any person as a slave.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● The Charter Act Of 1833 ensured the decline of the slavery that existed in British India. The Governor General was ordered to take some action to end the slavery that lived in British India. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The Indian Slavery Act, 1843, also known as Act V of 1843, was an act passed in British India under East India Company rule, which outlawed many economic transactions associated with slavery. The Act stated that the sale of any person as a slave was banned and anyone buying or selling slaves would be booked under with an offence carrying strict punishment. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the Chamber of Princes, consider the following statements:
1. It was established by the Indian Councils Act, 1909 to advise the British Government.
2. Princes of all the Princely States in India were directly represented in the Chamber.
3. Its annual meeting was presided over by the Queen of Great Britain.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The Chamber of Princes (Narendra Mandal) was an institution established in 1920 by a royal proclamation of King-Emperor George V to provide a forum in which the rulers of the princely states of India could voice their needs and aspirations to the colonial Government of British India. It survived until the end of the British Raj in 1947.
● When Montagu (Secretary of State for India from 1917 to 1922) and Chelmsford (Viceroy of India between 1916 and 1921) initiated their inquiry about post-world war I constitutional reforms, the princes raised among other issues the demand for a Chamber of Princes, an advisory committee and the right of direct access to the Government of India. The Government of India Act of 1919 provided for a 120-member Chamber of Princes to advise the British Raj on all matters relating to the States and their relationship with the paramount power. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The Chamber first met on 8 February 1921 and initially consisted of 120 members. Of those, 108 from the more significant states were members in their own right, while the remaining twelve seats were for the representation of a further 127 states. That left 327 minor states, which were unrepresented. Also, some of the more important rulers like the Maratha-ruled states of Baroda State, Gwalior State and Holkar State declined to join it. So, statement 2 is not correct
○ The composition of its membership remained however a major contentious issue, as ultimately it was decided that all princes with the 11 gun salutes would elect twelve representatives from among themselves. The Chamber formally broke the physical as well as political isolation of the princes.
● The Viceroy of India presided over the annual meeting of the Chamber of Princes. So, statement 3 is not correct
So, none of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to Indian support to the British in the Second World War (1939-45):
1. Jawaharlal Nehru advocated no Indian participation in the war till India itself was free.
2. Mahatma Gandhi supported the British war efforts.
3. Socialists thought it was the ideal time to launch a civil disobedience movement.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Though the Indian National Congress (INC) did not like the unilateral action of the British of drawing India into the Second World War (1939-45) without consulting the Indians, it decided to support the war effort conditionally. The Indian offer to cooperate in the war effort had two basic conditions:
○ After the war, a constituent assembly should be convened to determine the political structure of a free India.
○ Immediately, some form of a genuinely responsible government should be established at the Centre.
● Jawaharlal Nehru believed that justice was on the side of Britain, France and Poland, but he also believed that Britain and France were imperialist powers, and that “the war was the result of the inner contradictions of capitalism maturing since the end of World War I”. He, therefore, advocated no Indian participation till India itself was free. However, at the same time, no advantage was to be taken of Britain’s difficulty by starting an immediate civil disobedience movement. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Mahatma Gandhi, who had all sympathy for Britain in this war because of his total dislike of the fascist ideology, advocated unconditional support to the Allied powers. He made a clear distinction between the democratic nations of Western Europe and the totalitarian Nazis and fascists. He said that he was not willing to embarrass the British government during the war. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Subhash Chandra Bose and other socialists, such as Acharya Narendra Dev and Jayaprakash Narayan, who had been invited by the Congress to attend the Wardha meeting (1942) so that different opinions could be discussed, had no sympathy for either side in the war. In their opinion, the war was being fought by imperialists on both sides; each side wanted to protect its colonial possessions and gain more territories to colonise, so neither side should be supported by the nationalists. They thought it was the ideal time to launch a civil disobedience movement, to thus take advantage of the situation and snatch freedom from Britain. So, statement 3 is correct. So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the National Archives of India, consider the following statements:
1. It was established in 1891 as the Imperial Record Department.
2. It works as an attached office under the Union Ministry of Education.
3. The Director General of Archives has been given the mandate to implement the Public Records Act, 1993.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The National Archives of India is the custodian of the records of enduring value of the Government of India. Established in 1891 at Calcutta (Kolkata) as the Imperial Record Department, it is the biggest archival repository in South Asia. It has a vast corpus of records viz., public records, private papers, oriental records, cartographic records and microfilms, which constitute an invaluable source of information for scholars-administrators and users of archives. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The National Archives of India is an attached office under the Union Ministry of Culture. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● The Director General of Archives, heading the Department has been given the mandate for the implementation of the Public Records Act, 1993 and the rules made there under, the Public Records Rules, 1997 for the management, administration and preservation of public records in the Ministries, Departments, Public Sector undertakings etc. of the Central Government. So, statement 3 is correct. So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Festival
 State
Shigmo Mela
 Sikkim
Pakke Paga Hornbill Festival
 Nagaland
Wanchuwa Festival
 Assam
Mahamastakabhisheka
 Gujarat
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?

so.t:
● Shigmo Mela: It is a form of Holi celebration in Goa. In addition to playing with colours, it is planned as a huge carnival with traditional folk music and street dances. There are two methods to celebrate the Shigmo: the rural community celebrates the "Dhakto Shigmo," which is small in nature, while everyone else celebrates the "Vhadlo Shigmo," which is larger in nature. So, pair 1 is not correctly matched.
● Pakke Paga Hornbill Festival: It is celebrated in Arunachal Pradesh with the aim to recognise the role played by the Nyishi tribe in conserving hornbills in Pakke Tiger Reserve. Other objectives were to raise alternative sources of income for the region and to create awareness in the rest of India about the wonders of PTR and its surrounding areas. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
● Wanchuwa festival: It is celebrated by Tiwa tribes of Assam. It is celebrated once in every five years for a good harvest and the welfare of the people. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
● Mahamastakabhisheka: It is the abhisheka or anointment of the massive statue of Gomateshwara also known as Bahubali that stands atop the Vindhyagiri Hill in the town of Shravanabelagola of Hassan District, Karnataka. The statue of Lord Bahubali, the son of the first Jain tirthankara, was carved from a single rock by Chamundaraya, who was the Ganga Dynasty's minister and commander, in 981 AD. The festival is celebrated once in 12 years.
So, pair 4 is not correctly matched. So, only one of the above pairs is correctly matched.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

q.t: The National Essential Diagnostics List (NEDL) that contains the minimum diagnostic tests that should be available at healthcare facilities is maintained by:

so.t:
● The National Essential Diagnostics List (NEDL) lists the essential and most basic tests that should be available at various levels of healthcare facilities in the country, including at the village level, in sub health centres, health and wellness centres, and primary health centres.
● The development and implementation of an NEDL was recommended by the World Health Organisation(WHO), to facilitate the availability of in-vitro diagnostics across the various tiers of the healthcare pyramid.
○ India’s first NEDL was released by the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) in 2019 to make the availability of diagnostics an essential component of the healthcare system.
● The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) defined essential diagnostic tests as those that satisfy the priority health care needs of the population and are selected with due regard to disease prevalence and public health relevance, evidence of efficacy and accuracy, and comparative cost-effectiveness.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: It is the national geo-portal that allows users to view varieties of geo-spatial and thematic maps.
It is an interstellar device to capture cosmic sound from other solar systems.
It refers to the communication module onboard India's future space station.
It is a stabilised base to perform in-orbit experiments.

q.t: Which one of the following statements is correct with reference to the POEM Platform of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)?

so.t:
PSLV Orbital Experimental Module (POEM):
● It is a platform that will help perform in-orbit experiments using the final, and otherwise discarded, stage of ISRO’s workhorse rocket, the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV). The PSLV is a four-stage rocket where the first three spent stages fall back into the ocean, and the final stage (PS4), after launching the satellite into orbit, ends up as space junk. However, in PSLV-C53 mission, the spent final stage will be utilised as a “stabilised platform” to perform experiments.
● POEM has a dedicated Navigation Guidance and Control (NGC) system for attitude stabilisation, which stands for controlling the orientation of any aerospace vehicle within permitted limits. The NGC will act as the platform’s brain to stabilise it with specified accuracy.
● POEM will derive its power from solar panels mounted around the PS4 tank, and a Lithium-ion battery. It will navigate using four sun sensors, a magnetometer, gyros & Navigation with Indian Constellation (NavIC).
● It carries dedicated control thrusters using Helium gas storage. It is enabled with a telecommand feature.
● POEM platform has standard interfaces and packaging for power generation, telemetry, tele-command stabilisation, orbital-station keeping and orbital maneuvering. This allows space agencies and private companies to design, develop and validate experimental orbital payloads.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to Plasma waves:
1. They are short-time scale fluctuations in electric and magnetic field observations.
2. Some of the plasma waves act as a cleaning agent for the Earth’s radiation belt.
3. They are often observed in the Earth’s magnetosphere.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Recently scientists have examined the existence frequency of high plasma waves in the Martian plasma environment by making use of the high-resolution electric field data from the Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution Mission (MAVEN) spacecraft of NASA.
● Plasma waves are identified as the short-time scale fluctuations in the electric and magnetic field observations. These plasma waves play an important role in the energization and transport of the charged particles in the Earth’s magnetosphere. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Some of the plasma waves like electromagnetic ion cyclotron waves act as a cleaning agent for the Earth’s radiation belt, which is hazardous to our satellites. Knowing this scenario, researchers are curious to understand the existence of various plasma waves in the vicinity of unmagnetized planets like Mars. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Plasma waves can occur in both low and high frequencies, and their characteristics depend on various factors, including the properties of the plasma medium in which they propagate. These waves are often observed in the Earth’s magnetosphere, a magnetic field cavity around the Earth. So, statement 3 is correct. So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Judiciary from the executive and the legislative

q.t: The Directive Principles of State Policy under the Constitution of India explicitly provide for the separation of:

so.t:
● Article 50 of the Constitution of India under the Directive Principles of State Policy provides that the State shall take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State. ● The Criminal Procedure Code (1973) separated the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State
. Before this separation, the district authorities like the collector, the sub-divisional officer, the tehsildar and so on used to exercise judicial powers along with the traditional executive powers. After the separation, the judicial powers were taken away from these executive authorities and vested in the hands of district judicial magistrates who work under the direct control of the state high court.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following are administered/regulates by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)?
1. The Patents Act, 1970
2. The Trade Marks Act, 1999
3. Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999
4. The Copyright Act, 1957
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPITT) is a department under the Union Ministry of Commerce and Industry. Its role is to promote industrial development of the country by facilitating investment in new and upcoming technology, accelerate Foreign Direct Investment and support a balanced development of industries & trade. The following legislations are administered by the Department:
○ The Industries (Development and Regulation) Act, 1951
○ The Explosives Act, 1884
○ The Inflammable Substances Act, 1952
○ The Boilers Act, 1923
○ The Patents Act, 1970. So, point 1 is correct
○ The Copyright Act, 1957. So, point 4 is correct.
○ The Trade Marks Act, 1999. So, point 2 is correct.
○ The Design Act, 2000
○ The National Institute of Design Act, 2014
○ The Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999. So, point 3 is correct.
○ The Semiconductor Integrated Circuits Layout-Design Act, 2000
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t: In the context of Indian Polity, the power to create the Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) lists rests with which one of the following?

so.t:
● Article 341 and Article 342 of the Constitution of India define who would be Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) with respect to any State or Union Territory. These Articles vest the powers of notifying SC and ST lists with the President of India and of creating the lists with Parliament.
● Article 341(2) and Article 342(2) of the Constitution empowers the Parliament to include in or exclude from the list of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the Point of Order in the Parliament, consider the following statements:
1. It is related to the interpretation of the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in the House.
2. It can be raised only during the Zero Hour in the House.
3. The Presiding Officer of the House is the final authority to decide whether a point raised is a Point of Order.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Point of Order:
● It relates to the interpretation or enforcement of the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in the House or convention or such Articles of the Constitution as regulate the business of the House and raises a question which is within the cognizance of the Speaker. So, statement 1 is correct.
● It can be raised in both the Houses of Parliament. It may be raised in relation to the business before the House during the interval between the termination of one item of business and the commencement of another. It is not necessarily raised during the Zero Hour. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● A Member of Parliament can formulate a Point of Order and the Presiding Officer of the House shall decide whether the point raised is a Point of Order and if so, give the decision thereon, which is final.
So, statement 3 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following statements is/are correct with reference to court martial in India?
1. General Court Martial (GCM) is the highest level of military court martial in India.
2. The President of India cannot pardon the sentence given by a court martial.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● A court martial is a type of military court that is empowered to try members of the armed forces for offences committed under military law. A court martial is typically composed of military officers who serve as both judge and jury.
● General Court Martial (GCM) is the highest level of military court martial in India, and it can be convened to try the most serious offences, including those that carry the death penalty. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The accused can approach the Armed Forces Tribunal, which can suspend the sentence. The President of India, under Article 72 of the Constitution of India, can use her/his powers to pardon, reprieve, respite or remission of punishment or sentence given by a court martial.
So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following:
1. Willful disobedience of an order of a court
2. Comment on the administrative side of the judiciary
3. Publication of a matter that interferes with the administration of justice
How many of the above are considered civil contempt under the Contempt of Court Act, 1971?

so.t:
Contempt of Court:
● Both the Supreme Court and the High Courts in India have the power to punish for contempt of court, either with simple imprisonment or with fine or both.
● The term ‘Contempt of Court’ has not been defined by the Constitution of India. However, it has been defined by the Contempt of Court Act of 1971.
Civil Contempt Vs Criminal Contempt:
● The Contempt of Court can be ‘civil’ or ‘criminal’. Civil Contempt is the willful disobedience of any judgement, order, writ or other process of a court or willful breach of an undertaking given to a court.
So, point 1 is correct.
● Criminal Contempt, as defined by the Contempt of Court Act of 1971, is the publication of any matter or doing an act which:
○ Scandalises or lowers the authority of a court (The expression “scandalising the court” has not been defined); or
○ Prejudices or interferes with the due course of a judicial proceeding; or
○ Interferes or obstructs the administration of justice in any other manner. So, point 3 is not correct.
● However, the following are ‘not’ considered ‘Contempt of Court’:
○ Innocent publication and distribution of some matter
○ Fair and accurate report of judicial proceedings
○ Fair and reasonable criticism of judicial acts.
○ Comment on the administrative side of the judiciary. So, point 2 is not correct So, only one of the above is considered civil contempt under the Contempt of Court Act, 1971.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct and Statement–II is the correct explanation for Statement–I
Both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct and Statement–II is not the correct explanation for Statement–I
Statement–I is correct but Statement–II is incorrect
Statement–I is incorrect but Statement–II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: The Constitution of India empowers the State Government to remit any sentence of a person convicted under the Central or State laws.
Statement-II: Prison is a State subject under the State List of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution of India.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
● According to the Constitution of India, the authority to grant pardons, as well as to suspend, remit or commute sentences imposed by the courts, is vested exclusively in the President (under Article 72) and the Governor of the State (under Article 161). Notably, this authority is not delegated to the State government by the Constitution. Instead, Section 432(1) of the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), 1973, specifies that the competent Government, when an individual is sentenced for an offence, may, at any time, suspend the execution of the sentence or remit either the entire punishment or a portion of the imposed punishment. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The State List of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution of India designates 'Prison' as a State subject. Consequently, the management and administration of Prisons fall exclusively in the domain of the State Governments and are governed by the Prisons Act, 1894 and the Prison Manuals of the respective State Governments. Thus, States have the primary role, responsibility and authority to change the current prison laws, rules and regulations. So, statement 2 is correct. So, Statement–I is incorrect but Statement–II is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to surrogacy in India, consider the following statements:
1. The surrogate must have a child of her own.
2. The surrogate and the couple must have Aadhaar cards.
3. No payment or inducement can be offered to the surrogate.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Under the Surrogacy (Regulation) Act, 2021, the woman and man, willing to have a child through surrogacy, have to be between 25 and 50 years of age. They should not have had a child, either naturally conceived, adopted or born out of surrogacy.
● Also, the surrogate has to be eligible too. She has to be between 25 and 35 years of age; be married and have a child of her own. She should also be a first-time surrogate. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The law also says the surrogate and the couple would have to have their Aadhaar cards linked. It would help make the biometrics of the persons in the arrangement traceable, thereby reducing the scope of malpractice significantly. So, statement 2 is correct.
● The Act aims that the practice of surrogacy has to be altruistic. Hence, it has banned commercialisation of the surrogacy. Furthermore, no payment, reward, benefit, fees, remuneration, or inducement can be offered to the surrogate, her dependents, or her representative. So, statement 3 is correct. So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following features of the Constitution of India have been taken from the Government of India Act, 1935?
1. Procedure established by Law
2. Public Service Commissions
3. Suspension of Fundamental Rights during emergency
4. Office of the Governor
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● The Constitution of India is one of the most comprehensive and meticulously crafted constitutions, drawing upon a variety of sources, including historical documents, legal precedents and constitutions of various other countries, as well as the Government of India Act of 1935.
● The concept of "Procedure Established by Law" enumerated in Article 21 (Protection of Life and Personal Liberty) of the Indian Constitution is influenced by the Japanese Constitution. So, point 1 is not correct.
● The provisions related to the features such as the Federal Scheme, Office of the Governor, Judiciary, Public Service Commissions, Emergency Provisions, and administrative details have been derived from the Government of India Act, 1935. So, points 2 and 4 are correct.
● The provision for the suspension of Fundamental Rights during an emergency declared under Article 352 of the Constitution draws inspiration from the Weimar Constitution of Germany. So, point 3 is not correct.
● The provisions related to the features namely Parliamentary government, Rule of Law, legislative procedure, single citizenship, cabinet system, prerogative writs, parliamentary privileges and bicameralism have been borrowed from the British Constitution.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding the communist movement in Punjab:
1. The Communist Party of India had significant presence in Punjab during the 1940s-1950s, particularly among the urban working class and peasantry
2. Master Tara Singh collaborated with communist leaders during the Punjabi Suba movement
3. The Kirti Kisan Party, founded by Sohan Singh Josh, played an important role in mobilizing peasants before merging with the CPI
4. Punjab has elected Communist Party members to the state legislative assembly in multiple elections post-independence
Which of the above statements are correct?

so.t: Explanation: Statements 1, 3, and 4 are correct, making option B the right answer. The Communist Party of India (CPI) did have notable presence in Punjab during the 1940s-1950s, particularly among industrial workers in cities like Amritsar, Ludhiana, and Jalandhar, and among the rural peasantry facing exploitation by landlords. The party participated in various labor and peasant movements. Statement 3 is accurate—the Kirti Kisan Party (Revolutionary Workers and Peasants Party) was founded in 1928 by Sohan Singh Josh and other communist-leaning revolutionaries. This party played a significant role in organizing peasants against British colonial policies and feudal exploitation. It published the journal "Kirti" (Worker) in Punjabi, which became influential in spreading socialist and anti-colonial ideas. The Kirti Kisan Party eventually merged with the Communist Party of India in 1934-35, strengthening the communist movement in Punjab. Statement 4 is also correct—Punjab has elected communist party candidates to its legislative assembly in various elections after independence, though their numbers have never been large enough to form a government. The CPI and later CPI(M) have had pockets of support, particularly in certain constituencies with significant working-class populations. However, Statement 2 is incorrect and is the reason option B is correct rather than D. Master Tara Singh, as a leader of the Shiromani Akali Dal, did NOT collaborate with communist leaders during the Punjabi Suba movement. In fact, the Akali Dal's religious-political orientation and the communists' secular-socialist ideology often put them at odds. The Punjabi Suba movement was primarily led by Akali Dal with a focus on linguistic and religious identity, while communists generally opposed religious-based political mobilization. Understanding the communist movement in Punjab is important for comprehending the state's complex political history and the various ideological currents that influenced its post-independence development.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. Personal Laws in India do not come under the purview of Article 13 of the Constitution of India.
2. The Special Marriage Act, 1954 is a type of personal Law in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● Personal laws refer to a set of laws where an individual is governed in respect of matters relating to marriage, divorce, maintenance, adoption, inheritance, guardianship, succession, etc.
● Article 13(3), Clause (a) of the Constitution of India broadly defines 'law’ as any ordinance, order, bye law, rule, regulation, notification, custom or usage that have the force of law in the territory of India. It also covers the administrative orders by any executive officer. Personal laws are not covered within the ambit of law under this Article. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The Special Marriage Act of 1954 governs a civil marriage where the State sanctions the marriage rather than the religion and is hence considered a law under Article 13. It lets people register marriage even if they are of different religious faiths. In one of its judgements, the Supreme Court stated that ‘when a person solemnises marriage under this law, then the marriage is not governed by personal laws but by the Special Marriage Act’. It also stated that marriage celebrated under any other form can be registered under the Special Marriage Act if it satisfies the requirements mentioned under the law. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to the office of Public Prosecutor in India, consider the following statements:
1. S/He is appointed by the Central Government to conduct any prosecution or appeal proceeding on its behalf in the High Court.
2. The District Magistrate prepares a panel of names of persons to be appointed as Public Prosecutors in a district.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● A Public Prosecutor is a law officer who conducts criminal proceedings on behalf of the State or in the public interest.
● Under the Code of Criminal Procedure Act, 1973, for every High Court, the Central Government shall, after consultation with the High Court, appoint a Public Prosecutor and may also appoint one or more Additional Public Prosecutors, for conducting in such Court, any prosecution, appeal or other proceeding on behalf of the Central Government. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The District Magistrate shall, in consultation with the Sessions Judge, prepare a panel of names of persons, who are, in his opinion fit to be appointed as Public Prosecutors or Additional Public Prosecutors for the district. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Declining sex ratio, drug abuse crisis, youth unemployment, and outmigration - all reflecting socioeconomic distress
Groundwater depletion, stubble burning, debt crisis, and cancer belt in Malwa region - all stemming from unsustainable agricultural practices
Religious polarization, Khalistan issue, communal tensions, and political instability - all rooted in identity politics
Industrial decline, poor infrastructure, corruption in administration, and lack of foreign investment - all indicating governance failures

q.t: A researcher studying Punjab's contemporary challenges would need to understand the interlinkages between various issues. Which of the following represents the MOST interconnected set of challenges facing Punjab today?

so.t: Explanation: Option B represents the most scientifically and causally interconnected set of challenges, making it the best answer. These issues form a clearly linked chain of causation stemming from the Green Revolution's legacy and current agricultural practices. Groundwater depletion is directly caused by the intensive wheat-paddy cultivation cycle requiring continuous irrigation, particularly for water-intensive paddy during non-monsoon seasons. Punjab extracts groundwater far beyond its recharge capacity. Stubble burning is directly related to this same wheat-paddy cycle—farmers burn paddy stubble to quickly prepare fields for wheat cultivation, creating severe air pollution. The debt crisis is interconnected because farmers invest heavily in inputs (seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, electricity for tube wells) without corresponding increases in profitability due to stagnant productivity and rising input costs, while water scarcity increases costs further. The "cancer belt" in the Malwa region (districts like Bathinda, Mansa, Muktsar) is linked to excessive use of chemical pesticides and fertilizers, and possibly contamination of groundwater with heavy metals and chemicals. All these issues trace back to the unsustainable agricultural model perpetuated by MSP-driven monoculture. While option A identifies real problems (declining sex ratio, drug crisis, unemployment, migration), these are somewhat diverse socioeconomic issues with multiple causative factors not as tightly interconnected. Option C focuses on political issues that, while significant, have varied historical roots and aren't as systematically linked. Option D identifies governance challenges that are real but again represent separate administrative issues rather than an integrated crisis. Punjab's environmental-agricultural crisis represented in option B is perhaps the most critical contemporary challenge requiring urgent policy intervention, including crop diversification, water management, promotion of natural farming, and moving away from the wheat-paddy monoculture. Understanding these interconnections is crucial for UPSC/PCS candidates analyzing Punjab's development challenges.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to coral bleaching:
1. If the temperatures are high, the coral and microalgae become light-sensitive.
2. The chemical compound called reactive oxygen causes the coral to expel its microalgae.
3. Once the corals are bleached, they cannot recover naturally.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
● Coral bleaching is a phenomenon that occurs when the white skeleton of the corals becomes visible after the microalgae that live inside their translucent tissues are expelled.
● Coral bleaching is generally linked only to ocean temperatures. The process is a product of the interaction between high temperatures and sunlight. If the temperatures are high enough, the coral and microalgae become more light-sensitive. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Excessive sunlight harms the microalgae which, in turn, results in the production of chemical compounds called reactive oxygen. These compounds are harmful to many species and force the coral to expel its microalgae. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Clouds provide a protective barrier for coral reefs. Under a low emissions scenario, increased cloudiness improves coral's resilience and they get more time to recover from the impacts of rising temperatures.
● The coral reefs can recover from bleaching events, but the process requires a considerable amount of time. However, cloud cover helps them to recover faster. So, statement 3 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following polymers:
1. Polypropylene
2. Polyvinyl Chloride
3. Polystyrene
4. Polycarbonate
How many of the above are non-recyclable in nature?

so.t:
● A polymer is any of a class of natural or synthetic substances composed of very large molecules, called macromolecules, which are multiples of simpler chemical units called monomers.
● The difference in the recyclability of plastic types can be down to how they are made; thermoset plastics contain polymers that form irreversible chemical bonds and cannot be recycled, whereas thermoplastics can be re-melted and re-molded. The lower the resin code, the more likely the plastic type is to be easily recyclable, and vice-versa.
○ Resin codes or Resin Identification Codes (RICs) are numbers found on the bottom of some rigid plastic packaging. They are used to identify the type of plastic resin used to make a product, such as a plastic bottle, container, or jug.
● The general rule of thumb is, the lower the resin code, the more likely the plastic type is to be easily recyclable.
● The most commonly recycled plastics are:
○ Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET) – water bottles and plastic trays
○ High Density Polyethylene (HDPE) – milk cartoons and shampoo bottles
○ Polypropylene (PP) – margarine tubs and ready-meal trays. So, point 1 is not correct.
● Somewhat recyclable plastics (at specialist facilities) include:
○ Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC) – piping. So, point 2 is not correct.
○ Low Density Polyethylene (LDPE) – food bags
○ Polystyrene (PS) – plastic cutlery. So, point 3 is not correct.
● The difference in the recyclability of plastic types can be down to how they are made; thermoset plastics contain polymers that form irreversible chemical bonds and cannot be recycled, whereas thermoplastics can be re-melted and re-molded. Examples of non-recyclable plastics include bioplastics, composite plastic, plastic-coated wrapping paper and polycarbonate. Well known non-recyclable plastics include cling film and blister packaging. So, point 4 is correct.
So, only one of the above is non-recyclable in nature.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding World War II.
1. In the Pacific, the war turned against Japan during the Battle of Midway.
2. The tide of the war in Europe shifted with the Soviet victory at the Battle of Stalingrad.
Which of the following statements given above is/are correct?

so.t: Explanation: During the Battle of Midway (3–6 June 1942), the war in the Pacific turned against Japan. It was an American victory that destroyed the first-line carrier force of Japan and together with the Battle of Guadalcanal, ended the ability of Japan to prosecute an offensive war.

q.t:
Which of the following leaders led the Allied powers in World War II?
1. Winston Churchill
2. Joseph Stalin
3. Harry S. Truman
Choose the correct answer

so.t: Explanation: In World War II, the Allied powers were led by Winston Churchill (United Kingdom); Joseph Stalin (Soviet Union); Charles de Gaulle (France); Franklin D. Roosevelt, and Harry S. Truman (United States).

opt.t: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: Mercury is closer to Earth on average than Venus.
Statement-II: Mercury has the smallest orbit of any planet in the solar system.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
Explanation:
● Venus is considered the closest planet to Earth. The shortest approach is 0.28 Astronomical Unit (AU) or 25 million miles. However, Venus is not the closest planet on average. ○ 1 Astronomical Unit (AU) is the average distance between the Earth and the Sun: 93 million miles (150 million km).
● Mercury is closest to Earth on average i.e., Mercury spends more time closer to Earth because it orbits the Sun more closely. Mercury has the smallest orbit of any planet in the solar system. In fact, by this approach, Mercury is the closest neighbour of all planets in our solar system. So, statements 1 and 2 are correct. So, both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement I.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Match the following in the term of Thinkers and Ideas.
List-I (Thinkers)
 List-II (Ideas)
a. John Locke
 1. Partyless democracy
b. Hobbes
 2. Consent Theory
c. Jaya Prakash Narain
 3. Neo-liberalism
d. Robert Nozick
 4. Absolute Sovereignty

so.t:
Here is the explanation for the matching of thinkers to their ideas in the given question:
• John Locke: Known for the "Consent Theory" which states that the legitimacy of the government is based on the consent of the governed. This matches with option 2.
• Hobbes: Advocated for "Absolute Sovereignty" where a strong, absolute authority is needed to avoid chaos and maintain order. This matches with option 4.
• Jaya Prakash Narain: Famous for advocating for "Partyless democracy," which is a system where governance is without political parties. This matches with option 1.
• Robert Nozick: Known for his theory of "Neo-liberalism" focusing on minimal state intervention and individual liberties. This matches with option 3.
So, the correct matching is:
a-2, b-4, c-1, d-3

opt.t: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true

q.t:
Assertion (A): In a pressure cooker food is cooked above boiling point.
Reason (R): Boiling point of water increases as the pressure increases.

so.t:
Exp:
Both the Assertion (A) and the Reason (R) are true, and Reason (R) correctly explains Assertion (A). In a pressure cooker, food cooks above the normal boiling point of water because the boiling point increases as the pressure inside the cooker rises. This higher temperature leads to faster cooking.
Science Behind Pressure Cookers
A pressure cooker is a sealed cooking vessel that traps steam produced from boiling water inside. As the cooker is heated, the water begins to boil and turns into steam, but because the vessel is sealed, this steam cannot escape and builds up high internal pressure.
How Pressure Affects Boiling Point
• Under normal conditions, water boils at 100°C (212°F) at standard atmospheric pressure.
• In a pressure cooker, the trapped steam raises the pressure inside, often reaching 15 psi (about 1 bar) above atmospheric pressure.
• This increase in pressure raises the boiling point of water to about 121°C (250°F).
• As a result, food inside the cooker cooks at a much higher temperature than is possible in a regular pot, which speeds up cooking dramatically and helps tenderize tough foods.
Why This Works
• As temperature increases in a fixed-volume vessel (the pressure cooker), gas laws (Gay-Lussac's Law and the Ideal Gas Law) dictate that pressure must also increase.
• The higher temperature of the steam means more thermal energy is available, so food cooks faster.
• Cooking times can be reduced to about a quarter or a half of the time compared to conventional boiling.

q.t: The phenomenon of mirage occurs due to which one of the following?

so.t:
The phenomenon of a mirage occurs due to the total internal reflection of light (option d).
When light travels from denser (cooler) air to rarer (hotter) air near the ground, it bends away from the normal. At a certain angle (called the critical angle), the light gets totally reflected back into the denser layer, causing the observer to perceive an image (often, an illusion of water) that is not actually present. This is why mirages are commonly seen on hot roads or deserts.
Here's a brief explanation of each concept in the options:
(a) Polarization of light: This is a property of transverse waves where the vibrations (electric field in light) are restricted to a single plane. Unpolarized light vibrates in multiple planes; polarization makes it vibrate in just one.
(b) Dispersion of light: Dispersion is the phenomenon where light separates into its component colors (like a rainbow) because different wavelengths refract at different angles when passing through a medium.
(c) Diffraction of light: Diffraction refers to the bending and spreading of light waves when they pass around an obstacle or through a narrow slit, causing interference patterns.
(d) Total internal reflection of light: This occurs when light traveling in a denser medium hits the boundary of a rarer medium at an angle greater than the critical angle, causing all the light to be reflected back internally. This principle explains mirages.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No.
 Dam in News
 State
1
 Chungthang Dam
 Sikkim
2
 Dibang Multipurpose Project
 Arunachal Pradesh
3
 Lakhwar-Vyasi Dam Project
 Uttarakhand
4
 Ratle Hydroelectric Project
 Himachal Pradesh
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?

so.t:
● The Chungthang dam in Sikkim is a part of the 1,200-megawatt (MW) Teesta Stage III Hydro Electric Project. It collapsed in 2023 due to a glacial lake outburst flood (GLOF) in the South Lhonak Lake. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● The Dibang multipurpose project in Arunachal Pradesh is expected to be the highest dam in India once completed. It is aimed at preventing flooding in downstream areas. It is located on the Dibang River, a tributary of the Brahmaputra River. So, pair 2 is correctly matched.
● The Lakhwar-Vyasi dam project in the Upper Yamuna basin in Uttarakhand is often in the news due to environmental concerns. It is a 300 MW hydroelectric project (HEP) with a huge 40-kilometre-long reservoir on the river Yamuna near Lohari village in Dehradun. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
● India is constructing seven new dams in Kishtwar in Jammu and Kashmir – Bursar Dam, Pakal Dul, Kwar Dam, Kiru Dam, Kirthai-I, Kirthai II and Ratle hydroelectric project (930 MW). Ratle Hydroelectric project is located on the Chenab River. So, pair 4 is not correctly matched.
So, only three of the above pairs are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: It is caused by gravitational forces arising in the interactions between celestial objects like stars and gas clouds.
It is the tide caused as a result of the Earth’s gravitational pull on the Milky Way.
It is the gravity of the Sun that leads to periodic changes in the Milky Way's shape and structure.
It is a group of space debris that has human-made objects in space that are no longer useful.

q.t: Which one of the following statements best describes the term ‘galactic tide’?

so.t:
Explanation:
Galactic tides are caused by gravitational forces within a galaxy, arising in the interactions between celestial objects like stars and gas clouds. These tidal forces influence various aspects of a galaxy’s evolution. They can reshape a galaxy structure by creating tidal tails and bridges, promoting star formation, and disrupting smaller star systems.
Effects of Galactic tides:
● These tidal forces influence various aspects of a galaxy’s evolution.
● They can reshape a galaxy structure by creating tidal tails and bridges, promoting star formation and disrupting smaller star systems.
● They also disrupt the orbits of stars, leading to long-term changes in galactic structure.
● Galactic tides also have a say in how proximate galaxies do and don’t interact.
● Researchers have observed the closest galaxy to the Milky Way, the colossal Andromeda, and found that tidal streams near its edges could be signatures of dwarf galaxies that were later devoured.
● They also affect the supermassive black holes at galaxy centres, leading to events that change how these cosmic beasts interact with nearby stars.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the measurement of inflation:
1. The GDP deflator reflects the changes in consumption or investment patterns in an economy.
2. In India, the Consumer Price Index (Combined) is used as the GDP deflator.
3. The Wholesale Price Index (WPI) reckons only basic prices and does not include taxes and trade discounts.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The GDP deflator, also called implicit price deflator, is a measure of inflation. It is the ratio of the value of goods and services an economy produces in a particular year at current prices to that of prices that prevailed during the base year. It helps show the extent to which the increase in Gross Domestic Product (GDP) has happened on account of higher prices rather than an increase in output.
● Nominal GDP differs from real GDP as the former doesn’t include inflation, while the latter does. As a result, nominal GDP will most often be higher than real GDP in an expanding economy.
o GDP Price Deflator = (nominal GDP ÷ real GDP) x 100
● Changes in consumption patterns or introduction of goods and services are automatically reflected in the GDP deflator. This allows the GDP deflator to absorb changes to an economy’s consumption or investment patterns. So, statement 1 is correct.
● A Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures changes over time in the general level of prices of goods and services that households acquire for consumption. In India, a combination of Wholesale Price Index (WPI) and Consumer Price Index (CPI) is used as a deflator on a quarterly basis with a time lag of two months. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● While the CPI-based retail inflation looks at the price at which the consumer buys goods, the WPI tracks prices at the wholesale, or factory gate/mandi levels. WPI (2011-12) reckons only basic prices and does not include taxes, rebates/trade discounts, transport and other charges. So, statement 3 is correct.
o Between the wholesale price and the retail price, the difference essentially is the former only tracks basic prices devoid of transportation cost, taxes and the retail margin etc.
○ The wholesale price index basket has no representation of the services sector and all the constituents are only goods whose prices are captured at the wholesale/producer level. So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Protection of literary and artistic works
Protection of industrial patents and trademarks

q.t: The Berne Convention, 1886, recently in the news, is related to which one of the following?

so.t:
Explanation:
● The Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works, 1886 deals with the protection of works and the rights of their authors. It provides creators such as authors, musicians, poets, painters etc. with the means to control how their works are used, by whom, and on what terms.
● It is based on three basic principles and contains a series of provisions determining the minimum protection to be granted as well as special provisions available to developing countries that want to make use of them. It is a special agreement under the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO).
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following services are offered by the White Label ATMs (WLAs)?
1. Account information
2. Cash deposit
3. Regular bill payment
4. PIN change
5. Request for cheque book
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● An Automated Teller Machine (ATM) is a computerised machine that provides customers of banks the facility to access their accounts for dispensing cash and to carry out other financial & non-financial transactions without the need to visit the bank branch.
● ATMs set up, owned and operated by non-banks are called White Label ATMs (WLAs). Non-bank ATM operators are authorised under the Payment & Settlement Systems Act, 2007 by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI).
● For a customer, using a WLA is just like using an ATM of any bank. The rationale for allowing non-bank entities to set up WLAs has been to increase the geographical spread of ATMs for increased/enhanced customer service, especially in semi-urban / rural areas.
● WLAs provide banking services to the customers of banks in India, based on the cards (debit/credit/prepaid) issued by banks. In addition to dispensing cash, WLAs may offer many other services/facilities which inter-alia include:
o Account information. So, point 1 is correct.
o Cash deposit. So, point 2 is correct.
o Regular bill payment. So, point 3 is correct.
o Mini/Short statement generation
o PIN change. So, point 4 is correct.
o Request for cheque book. So, point 5 is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the velocity of money:
1. It is directly proportional to the money supply in the economy.
2. It decreases when consumers save more money than they spend.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● Velocity of money is a measurement of the rate at which money is exchanged in an economy. It is typically higher in expanding economies and lower in contracting economies.
● The velocity of money is usually measured as a ratio of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to a country's M1 or M2 money supply (V=PQ/M).
● There are several factors that can affect the velocity of money in an economy. These include:
○ Money supply and the velocity of money are inversely proportional. If the money supply in an economy falls short, then the velocity of money will rise, and vice versa. So, statement 1 is not correct.
○ It is affected by features of the monetary system, such as the availability of credit or electronic banking. When there are few barriers to transactions, the velocity of money tends to increase; when it is difficult to spend money, it decreases.
○ It is affected by the behavior of economic actors. When consumers prioritize saving rather than spending, the pace of transactions slows and the velocity of money declines. When consumers prioritize spending, the velocity of money speeds up. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

1. You have 120 minutes to complete the test.
2. The test contains a total of 100 questions And 200 Marks.
3. There is only one correct answer to each question. Click on the most appropriate option to mark it as your answer.
4. You will be awarded 2 for each correct answer.
5. There is no penalty for wrong answer.
6. You can change your answer by clicking on some other option.
7. You can unmark your answer by clicking on the “Clear Response” button.
8. A Number list of all questions appears at the right hand side of the screen. You can access the questions in any order within a section or across sections by clicking on the question number given on the number list.
9. You can use rough sheets while taking the test. Do not use calculators, log tables, dictionaries, or any other printed/online reference material during the test.
10. Do not click the button “Submit test” before completing the test. A test once submitted cannot be resumed.
